behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Failure depends on real AI/ML transcription+diarization quality (speaker labeling), which mocks can hide. The behavior is about label correctness in model output, not just transport or retry wiring. Rejected Workflow because behavior text targets a single STT output property, not multiple agentic handoff steps.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real audio sample with multiple speakers through the production STT API and assert returned transcript segments contain correct speaker labels that can be mapped to attendee identities.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: This is parser/extraction logic on transcript text and output structure; a local function-level test can catch real failures. Behavior text does not require real OS/network/subprocess or real model judgment. Rejected Integration because no explicit cross-module state handoff is required by this wording.
behaviors.test_description: Given transcript text fixtures containing explicit assignments, assert parser returns normalized assignee/task tuples in the exact summary-input schema.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on what the real LLM includes/omits in generated summary content across attendees; stubbed outputs can always pass and hide recall failures. Rejected Workflow because behavior asks for one output quality condition, not explicitly a multi-agentic stage sequence assertion.
behaviors.test_description: Use real transcript inputs with known per-attendee assignments, call the production summarization model, and assert each assigned attendee appears in at least one generated action item.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: Wording is about deterministic routing/mapping logic after decisions are already identified. This is local schema-placement behavior and can fail within one function/object boundary. Rejected Integration because behavior text does not require real module boundary failure, infra, or model output dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Feed pre-identified decision items into the routing function and assert they populate `decisions` schema fields and are absent from narrative buckets.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: This checks LLM content selection/placement quality in live output; deterministic fixtures cannot validate whether the model actually surfaces decisions correctly. Rejected Workflow because behavior does not require validating multiple agentic handoffs, only final model output quality condition.
behaviors.test_description: Provide transcripts with explicit key decisions to the real summarization model and assert those decisions appear in the output `decisions` section rather than only narrative text.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires multi-step sequencing (split -> per-segment processing -> merge) and final coherence across steps. Real failures often occur at agentic handoffs/ordering/merge boundaries over multiple model calls. Rejected Agentic because single-call validation cannot cover the required sequential multi-step pipeline behavior in the text.
behaviors.test_description: Run an end-to-end long recording through production chunking and sequential processing, verify segment order and merge correctness, and evaluate final merged summary coherence and completeness against known meeting facts.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: This is explicitly full-pipeline, multi-stage execution with real audio, STT, and LLM summarization in sequence. Minimum failure boundary is cross-stage orchestration plus agentic outputs, beyond any single module/system call. Rejected lower levels because behavior text directly requires end-to-end automated completion across all stages.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the production pipeline on a real meeting recording from upload through delivery, asserting successful automated completion and valid structured output with required sections.
